content.text:
REVIEW-FINDING: 5.6.1 / 5.6.3 WARN — RatingGateView.swift gates users by star selection before routing: 4-5 stars go directly to a hardcoded `itms-apps://...write-review` link (App/RatingGateView.swift:16-17), while <4 stars are diverted to a feedback form, and no `SKStoreReviewController`/`requestReview` API is used anywhere in the project.

Pierre: This is a textbook rating-gate dark pattern under 5.6.1/3.2.2(x) — filtering unhappy users away from the App Store while funneling happy ones to a custom write-review URL manipulates the review corpus and bypasses Apple's mandated `requestReview` API, which must show a native, non-filterable prompt to all users. The description.txt line "rate us 5 stars!" compounds this by explicitly soliciting a specific rating. Fix: replace the entire star-gate flow with `SKStoreReviewController.requestReview(in:)` shown unconditionally (no pre-filtering by sentiment), and remove the "rate us 5 stars" call-to-action from marketing copy.
